orbit_vault.role: Ethereum-side custody and validator-quorum manager for the Orbit Bridge. Held the 10 farmer (validator) addresses and ran the 7-of-10 ECDSA threshold check before unlocking tokens. Modelled by OrbitVault.sol extending the shared MockMultisig harness (N=10, K=7).

wbtc_ethereum.role: WBTC leg — ~$30M drained, the largest single asset.

attacker.role: Placeholder for the attacker EOA cluster that received the 5 large drain transactions in block 18900155. Real attribution: suspected Lazarus Group (DPRK) per Match Systems forensic analysis.

farmer_set_placeholders.role: Ten farmer (validator) ECDSA pubkeys. Production addresses were not publicly disclosed in their entirety; placeholders 0x..01..0x..0a stand in. Threshold 7-of-10 means any 7 of these signing off authorises a cross-chain unlock.

off_chain_compromise_note.reason: Same shape as Ronin (Sprint R) and Harmony (Sprint H): farmer (validator) private keys leaked off-chain. Match Systems' forensic analysis attributes this incident to Lazarus Group based on subsequent on-chain laundering patterns; the team's announcements stopped short of formal attribution.
off_chain_compromise_note.implication: Module 1 should infer the same `validator_set_authority` invariant pattern. Module 2's RAG retrieval has by this point seen Ronin (5/9) and Harmony (2/4) templates; Orbit's 7/10 (70%) is the highest threshold ratio in the off-chain set, which makes Module 2's reuse of the `key_compromise` template a sharper test of generalisation.
off_chain_compromise_note.modelled_by: OrbitVault extends MockMultisig (N=10, K=7). The compromise model is 'attacker is given 7 of the 10 farmer keys' — the highest absolute-count compromise in the dataset.

root_cause_summary.vulnerable_pattern: Bridge withdrawal authorisation requires 7-of-10 ECDSA signatures from a fixed farmer set. The on-chain check is correct.
root_cause_summary.verification_bypass: Off-chain: 7 of the 10 farmer private keys leaked. The compromise was highly concentrated — the attacker landed enough farmer infrastructure simultaneously to satisfy 7 keys in a single attacker identity. Operational details remain partially undisclosed by Ozys.
root_cause_summary.notes: 70% threshold-to-set ratio is the highest in the off-chain benchmark suite (Ronin 55.6%, Harmony 50%, Multichain 100% of a degenerate 1-of-1). Despite the high ratio, the absolute count required (7) was small enough relative to the operational concentration that a single attacker reaching enough farmer hosts cleared the bar.

exploit_characteristics.style: validator quorum collapse via concentrated farmer-key compromise
exploit_characteristics.notes: Five large drain transactions in block 18900155 (one block — much tighter than Ronin's 1-block double drain or Harmony's ~5-block 11-batch). The single-block pattern is consistent with all 7 keys being reachable from a single attacker host with pre-staged transactions ready to broadcast.

references.title: Ozys Official — Orbit Bridge Incident Statement
Match Systems — Orbit Bridge Hack Analysis
